Lithuanian long-distance runner

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/86/Z_Balciunaite.jpg/250px-Z_Balciunaite.jpg)](/wiki/File:Z_Balciunaite.jpg)

Živilė Balčiūnaitė at the 2010 [Golden Spike Ostrava](/wiki/Golden_Spike_Ostrava "Golden Spike Ostrava")

**Živilė Balčiūnaitė** (born 3 April 1979[[1]](#cite_note-espn-1) in [Vilnius](/wiki/Vilnius "Vilnius")) is a [Lithuanian](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania") Marathon runner.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C5%BDivil%C4%97_Bal%C4%8Di%C5%ABnait%C4%97&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Balčiūnaitė finished 4th at the [2006 European Athletics Championships](/wiki/2006_European_Athletics_Championships "2006 European Athletics Championships") in [Gothenburg](/wiki/Gothenburg "Gothenburg"). She also competed in the same event at the [2004 Olympics](/wiki/2004_Olympics "2004 Olympics"), finishing 14th. She finished 11th at the 2008 Olympics.[[1]](#cite_note-espn-1)

## Doping bans

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C5%BDivil%C4%97_Bal%C4%8Di%C5%ABnait%C4%97&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Doping bans")]

In April 2011, the [Athletics Federation of Lithuania](/wiki/Athletics_Federation_of_Lithuania "Athletics Federation of Lithuania") announced Balčiūnaitė has been banned for two years for a positive drug test and was stripped of her marathon gold medal from the 2010 European Athletics Championships.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Balčiūnaitė received an eight year ban in July 2016 by the Athletics Federation of Lithuania after testing positive for meldonium.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
